quote.text: In every walk with nature, one receives far more than he seeks.
quote.author: John Muir

fun_fact.text: Hummingbirds are the only birds that can fly backwards! Their wings can rotate in a full circle, allowing them to hover in place and even fly upside down briefly. The Pacific Northwest is home to the Rufous hummingbird, which migrates through the Olympic Peninsula each spring and summer.

short_read.title: The Secret Language of Trees
short_read.teaser: Scientists have discovered that trees communicate with each other through an underground network of fungi, sharing nutrients and warnings about pests. This "wood wide web" shows how forests are really communities working together.

medium_read.title: The Paprika Tradition of Hungary
medium_read.teaser: Paprika is more than just a spice in Hungary—it's a cultural treasure with centuries of history. Learn how this vibrant red powder became the heart of Hungarian cuisine and is celebrated in festivals across the country.

long_read.title: Olympic National Park: Where Wilderness Meets Wonder
long_read.teaser: From temperate rainforests to alpine peaks to wild coastline, Olympic National Park contains an incredible diversity of ecosystems right in your backyard. Discover the rare Roosevelt elk, ancient trees, and tide pool creatures that make this place magical.

port_angeles.title: Roosevelt Elk at Hurricane Ridge
port_angeles.description: June is a wonderful time to spot Roosevelt elk at Hurricane Ridge! These magnificent animals, named after President Theodore Roosevelt, are often seen grazing in the meadows in early morning or evening. They're the largest of the four surviving subspecies of elk in North America and are native only to the Pacific Northwest.

puzzle_1.question: A hummingbird's heart beats about ___ times per minute when active.
puzzle_1.correct_response: Exactly right! Can you imagine—that tiny heart working so incredibly fast to power those amazing wings!
puzzle_1.close_response: You're very close! A hummingbird's heart beats about 1,200 times per minute—that's 20 times every second!
puzzle_1.wrong_response: Those tiny hearts are amazing—they beat about 1,200 times per minute! That's what it takes to power those incredible hovering skills.
puzzle_1.learn_more_text: To support their incredibly fast metabolism, hummingbirds must visit hundreds of flowers each day and can consume up to twice their body weight in nectar daily.

puzzle_2.question: Which Hungarian word means 'butterfly'?
puzzle_2.correct_response: Wonderful! Pillangó is such a beautiful word for such a beautiful creature—it almost sounds like the fluttering of wings!
puzzle_2.wrong_response: The Hungarian word for butterfly is 'pillangó'—doesn't it sound lovely? (Virág means flower, madár means bird, and napfény means sunshine.)
puzzle_2.learn_more_text: Hungarian is a unique language in Europe, part of the Finno-Ugric family rather than the Indo-European family. It's most closely related to Finnish and Estonian, though even those languages are quite different.

puzzle_3.question: What is the Strait of Juan de Fuca named after?
options: A Greek explorer who sailed for Spain
puzzle_3.correct_response: Perfect! Juan de Fuca was actually a Greek navigator named Ioannis Phokas who sailed for Spain in the 1590s and claimed to have found this waterway. What a rich history right outside your window!
puzzle_3.wrong_response: The strait is named after Juan de Fuca, a Greek navigator (real name Ioannis Phokas) who sailed for Spain. He claimed to have discovered this passage in 1592, though historians debate whether he actually sailed here!
puzzle_3.learn_more_text: The Strait of Juan de Fuca is about 96 miles long and connects the Pacific Ocean to Puget Sound and the Salish Sea. It forms part of the international boundary between Canada and the United States.

game.title: Bubble Shooter
game.description: A relaxing bubble puzzle game — aim and pop matching colored bubbles. Easy to pick up, fun to play!
